title: RapidMeta Dermatology | Oral JAK Inhibitors NMA in Moderate-Severe Atopic Dermatitis v1.0
hero_h2: Oral JAK Inhibitors in Moderate-Severe Atopic Dermatitis (Network)
nyt_headline: The Oral JAKi Atopic Dermatitis Evidence

pico.pop: Adults (and adolescents in some trials) with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (vIGA-AD >=3, EASI >=16, BSA >=10%)
pico.int: Oral JAK inhibitor at the licensed/highest tested phase 3 dose (abrocitinib 200 mg QD, upadacitinib 30 mg QD, baricitinib 4 mg QD)
pico.out: vIGA-AD 0/1 with >=2-point reduction at week 12 (abrocitinib) or week 16 (upadacitinib, baricitinib) -- primary
pico.subgroup: Drug class (JAK1-selective vs pan-JAK), prior systemic, baseline EASI severity

real_data_entries.name: JADE-MONO-1
real_data_entries.group: Abrocitinib (JAK1) 200 mg QD vs placebo monotherapy; vIGA-AD 0/1 wk 12
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Simpson EL et al. Lancet 2020;396:255-266 (JADE-MONO-1).

real_data_entries.name: JADE-MONO-2
real_data_entries.group: Abrocitinib (JAK1) 200 mg QD vs placebo monotherapy; vIGA-AD 0/1 wk 12
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Silverberg JI et al. JAMA Dermatol 2020;156:863-873 (JADE-MONO-2).

real_data_entries.name: Measure Up 1
real_data_entries.group: Upadacitinib (JAK1) 30 mg QD vs placebo monotherapy; vIGA-AD 0/1 wk 16
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Guttman-Yassky E et al. Lancet 2021;397:2151-2168 (Measure Up 1).

real_data_entries.name: Measure Up 2
real_data_entries.group: Upadacitinib (JAK1) 30 mg QD vs placebo monotherapy; vIGA-AD 0/1 wk 16
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Guttman-Yassky E et al. Lancet 2021;397:2151-2168 (Measure Up 2).

real_data_entries.name: BREEZE-AD1
real_data_entries.group: Baricitinib (JAK1/2) 4 mg QD vs placebo monotherapy; vIGA-AD 0/1 wk 16
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Simpson EL et al. Br J Dermatol 2020;183:242-255 (BREEZE-AD1).

real_data_entries.name: BREEZE-AD2
real_data_entries.group: Baricitinib (JAK1/2) 4 mg QD vs placebo monotherapy; vIGA-AD 0/1 wk 16
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Simpson EL et al. Br J Dermatol 2020;183:242-255 (BREEZE-AD2).
